data.title: Sibanye Stillwater tries to free 260 miners trapped underground in South Africa mine incident
data.description:
Sibanye Stillwater (NYSE:SBSW) said Friday it is working to free 260 miners trapped underground at its Kloof gold mine in South Africa after the hoist system used to access the shaft was damaged in an accident.

Sibanye (NYSE:SBSW) said a "shaft incident" had occurred Thursday at the mine, one of South Africa's deepest; the company is digging for gold at its Kloof 7 shaft at depths of nearly two miles.

The Kloof mine, which accounts for 14% of Sibanye's (SBSW) total gold output, also operates two other shafts, and the company also mines platinum-group metals in South Africa and the U.S.

All the workers are safe and gathered at an assembly point where they were provided with food while efforts are being prepared to bring them out of the mine Friday afternoon.

data.title: Amazon shareholders reject proposal separate CEO and chair roles
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) shareholders rejected a proposal that would require the company to permanently separate the roles of the CEO and the board chair, with 82% voting against the proposal at Wednesday’s shareholder meeting.

The vote was initiated by the advocacy group, The Accountability Board, arguing that the company hasn’t codified this separation into policy “giving the board the ability to freely combine the roles at any time, and establish the leadership structure that it determines best supports its operations and the company at a particular time.”  

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) separated the roles of board chairman and CEO in 2021 when Jeff Bezos stepped down, handing over control of the company to CEO Andy Jassy. Bezos remained executive chairman of Amazon’s (NASDAQ:AMZN) board.

The board, however, argued that by maintaining the current structure, the board can “determine the right leadership for the company in light of our specific circumstances at any given time.”

“In light of our success through these various leadership structures, the board believes that shareholders are better served by the board retaining the ability to adapt to our evolving needs and implement the optimal leadership structure,” Amazon’s board said in its proxy statement, adding that “implementing the proposal would not change our leadership structure or further enhance the independent oversight the Board already exercises.”

data.title: Trump threatens 50% tariff on EU products starting in June
data.description:
Frustrated with the lack of progress in reaching a trade deal with the European Union, President Donald Trump said he'll recommend a 50% tariff on the EU starting June 1, 2025, according to his social media account.

"The European Union, which was formed for the primary purpose of taking advantage of the United States on TRADE, has been very difficult to deal with," he wrote in a post on Truth Social. "Their powerful Trade Barriers, Vat Taxes, ridiculous Corporate Penalties, Non-Monetary Trade Barriers, Monetary Manipulations, unfair and unjustified lawsuits against American Companies, and more, have led to a Trade Deficit with the U.S. of more than $250,000,000 [sic] a year, a number which is totally unacceptable. Our discussions with them are going nowhere!"

Note that the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative website said the U.S. total goods trade deficit with the EU was $235.6B in 2024, a 12.9% increased from 2023.

The president also repeated that no tariffs are charged on products "built or manufactured" in the U.S.

Earlier, Trump said that he'd place a 25% tariff rate on iPhones that aren't made in the U.S.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wolfspeed names David Emerson as COO
data.description:
• Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) said it has appointed David Emerson as its COO and executive vice president.
  • The firm added Emerson will be responsible for Wolfspeed’s operations, supply chain, and quality divisions.

data.title: PSQ Holding appoints James Rinn as CFO
data.description:
PSQ Holding (NYSE:PSQH) Friday  announced that James Rinn has been appointed Chief Financial Officer, effective June 1, 2025. 

Rinn, who has served as a board member and chair of the PublicSquare Audit Committee since July 2023, will succeed Brad Searle, who will transition to Senior Vice President of Finance for the Company’s Brands Division. 

Rinn brings over 30 years of accounting leadership at public and private companies, having held multiple CFO roles throughout his career.

data.title: Bank of America to redeem CHF-denominated notes
data.description:
Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) announced on Friday the redemption of all of the CHF 375M principal amount outstanding on its 0.2525% senior notes due June 12, 2026.

Shares were -2.36% to $42.29.

The banking giant will redeem the notes on June 12, 2025.

The redemption price will be equal to the optional redemption amount of CHF 5,000 per CHF 5,000 calculation amount, plus any accrued and unpaid interest.

The last day of trading on the SIX Swiss Exchange will be June 10.

data.title: Merus gains after mid-stage trial data for lead asset in head and neck cancer
data.description:
Shares of Merus N.V. (NASDAQ:MRUS) added ~16% in the premarket on Friday after the Dutch biotech posted interim data from a mid-stage trial designed to test its lead candidate, petosemtamab, in combination with Merck's (MRK) PD-1 inhibitor pembrolizumab in head and neck cancer.

Citing a Feb. 27 data cut, Merus (NASDAQ:MRUS) said petosemtamab with pembrolizumab, marketed as Keytruda by Merck (MRK), led to a confirmed overall response rate of 63% among 43 efficacy-evaluable patients.

While the study population hadn’t reached the median duration of response and median overall survival at the time of the data cut, the treatment combo had indicated a 79% overall survival rate at 12 months and a median progression-free survival of nine months.

The study tested Merus’ (NASDAQ:MRUS) bispecific antibody with Keytruda as a first-line option for PD-L1-positive recurrent or metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC).

“By essentially every metric, we believe these interim data are significantly better than pembrolizumab monotherapy, the control arm of our ongoing phase 3 trial,” said CEO Bill Lundberg. It “underscores the opportunity petosemtamab holds to become a new standard of care if approved in head and neck cancer," he added.

Based on data from 45 patients, the company said the drug combination was generally well tolerated among trial participants, even though all of them indicated treatment-emergent adverse events. 60% witnessed Grade 3 or higher TEAEs, and 20% experienced treatment-related TEAEs.

The company is scheduled to present the results on June 2 at the upcoming annual meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO).

data.title: Nasdaq futures fall sharply, yields slip as Trump threatens tariffs on Apple
data.description:
Stock index futures fell sharply on Friday, while yields slipped, as President Donald Trump said tariffs must be paid on iPhones not made in the U.S., while the focus also remained on the country's fiscal situation.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.47%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 7 basis points to 3.93%.

Apple (AAPL) shares fell 2.5% in premarket trading on Friday after Trump said the tech giant would have to pay 25% tariffs on iPhones not made in the U.S.

Wall Street's major averages ended largely flat on Thursday, while a bruising bond sell-off from the previous session eased up. The focus remained on Trump's “One Big Beautiful” tax bill, which was narrowly passed by the U.S. House.

U.S. Treasury yields stabilized on Thursday after soaring on Wednesday, as traders had dumped bonds amid concerns over the new tax bill's effects on the ballooning U.S. fiscal deficit.

“After a heavy selloff on Wednesday, markets began to stabilize again over the last 24 hours, despite investors’ ongoing fears about the fiscal situation,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

One factor helping U.S. assets to stabilize was some stronger U.S. economic data, which continued to point away from a recession, Allen added.

Administration comments suggest Trump is willing to increase taxes on U.S. consumers of EU-made products if the EU does not make unilateral concessions on tariffs, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

“Markets are unlikely to place too much weight on these threats given the president’s tendency to rapidly retreat on such issues, but the situation does (again) increase policy uncertainty,” Donovan added.

The economic calendar is quiet today with mainly the April new home sales on tap. Economists on average expect it to fall to 694K.

U.S. markets will be closed on Monday for Memorial Day.

data.title: V.F. Corp declares $0.09 dividend
data.description:
• V.F. Corp (NYSE:VFC) declares $0.09/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.96%
  • Payable June 18; for shareholders of record June 10; ex-div June 10.
  • See VFC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: IonQ signs MoU with KISTI to accelerate South Korea’s role in global quantum race
data.description:
• IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) Friday  unveiled the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with the Korea Institute of Science and Technology Information.
  • Memorandum aims to integrate IonQ’s quantum technology into South Korea’s high-performance computing infrastructure, fueling R&D, talent development, and ecosystem growth.
  • Under the terms of the MoU, IonQ and KISTI will collaborate in four key areas: advanced infrastructure access, education, talent and knowledge exchange as well as collaboration to expand market opportunities.
